summary: Backend-only testing of the new Brokers module (/api/brokers/*) and refactored /api/market/broker-status. 18/18 pytest assertions passed covering broker registry shape (5 brokers, angel_one configured, 4 coming_soon), auth-gating on /connect (401), public callback redirects (302 to /connect-broker?status=error with correct messages for missing/invalid state), unknown-broker safety (no 500), broker-status response shape, and the in-memory state-token state machine (create -> consume -> one-time-use -> expiry). All review-request acceptance criteria for the brokers feature itself are satisfied. HOWEVER, two regressions introduced by the angel_one_service refactor were discovered in backend logs and the legacy market-data hot path -- see issues below.

critical.issue: server.py still references `angel.auth_token` at lines 195, 198, 258, 261, 1069, 1112 but the refactored AngelOneService no longer exposes this attribute. Every market-data poll spams 'WARNING - Angel One API error for RELIANCE/TCS/INFY/...: AngelOneService object has no attribute auth_token' (~30 lines every 30s). Effect: even after a user successfully connects Angel One via the new publisher-login flow, live quotes/indices/stocks routes will AttributeError and fall through to the 'angel_one_unavailable' / simulated branch -- live-data integration is fully broken for /api/market/stocks, /api/market/indices, /api/market/quote.

server.py startup_event still calls angel.login() and logs 'Angel One auto-login failed - will use simulated data until manual login' as a WARNING on every boot because the refactored login() now always returns False by design. The review request explicitly states this warning must NOT appear at startup. Fix: remove the auto-login startup hook entirely (or replace with an info-level 'Angel One ready in publisher-login mode' log).

minor.issue: get_session_status() in angel_one_service.py returns 'auth_mode': 'publisher_login' but server.py's broker_status dict drops this field, so the UI can't tell which auth mode the server is in. Low impact -- add 'auth_mode' to the returned broker_status dict.

action_items: CRITICAL: Replace all `angel.auth_token` references in server.py (lines 195, 198, 258, 261, 1069, 1112) with a session lookup, e.g. `session = broker_session_manager.any_active_for_broker('angel_one')` and then call angel methods only if session is not None. Without this fix, no live market data will work even after a user connects.

HIGH: Remove (or downgrade to debug) the 'Angel One auto-login failed' WARNING in server.py startup_event() (around line 2004). The legacy login() intentionally always returns False under the new publisher-login model -- this is no longer a failure case.
Optional: expose `auth_mode` field on /api/market/broker-status so the UI can display 'Connect via publisher login' instead of legacy MPIN/TOTP language.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2018 lines -- well above the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting routers (brokers, market, arbitrage, auth) into separate modules under /app/backend/routers/.

broker_callback (server.py:1296) hard-codes the frontend redirect path '/connect-broker' as a same-origin relative URL. This works because ingress routes non-/api paths to the frontend, but it's fragile in deploys with a separate frontend host. Consider using REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL or a new FRONTEND_URL env var.

AngelOneProvider.handle_callback raises on missing auth_token but does not surface broker-returned `status=false&message=...` cleanly; the error reaches the user as the raw exception string. Consider mapping known error codes from Angel One.
session_manager.STATE_TTL is mutated in tests via direct attribute assignment -- fine for unit tests, but exposes mutable global state. Consider passing TTL into the constructor.
broker_session_manager.set() silently overwrites an existing session for (user_id, broker_id). For audit/UX it may be worth logging the overwrite.

Callback endpoint accepts query params only -- some brokers (Upstox/Zerodha) POST tokens or pass them in the URL fragment. The BrokerProvider.handle_callback signature should accept the full Request or both query & form params to be truly extensible.

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 brokers tests), but legacy market-data path broken by refactor -- see CRITICAL issues

test_credentials: Not required for this run -- all tested endpoints are either public or use the in-memory state machine. No real Angel One credentials were used (per request: do not attempt actual Angel One login).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The new /api/brokers/* surface area is solid (all 18 tests pass; redirects exactly match the spec). The remaining failures are NOT in the brokers module itself but in the legacy market-data plumbing (server.py:190-270, 1060-1115) which still references the removed `AngelOneService.auth_token` attribute and triggers AttributeError on every market data fetch. After the main agent fixes the auth_token references and the startup warning, re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_brokers.py` plus a quick `curl /api/market/stocks?symbols=RELIANCE` and verify backend.err.log no longer contains 'has no attribute auth_token' or 'auto-login failed'.
rca of the issue: Root cause: the angel_one_service.py refactor switched from per-instance state (`self.auth_token`, `self.refresh_token`, etc.) to delegating to BrokerSessionManager.any_active_for_broker('angel_one'). The refactor removed the `auth_token` attribute but left six callers in server.py untouched. Reproduction: `curl https://broker-integrator.preview.emergentagent.com/api/market/stocks?symbols=RELIANCE` then `tail /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log` -- you'll see `'AngelOneService' object has no attribute 'auth_token'` warnings. Mitigation: in server.py replace the pattern `if not angel.auth_token: angel.login(); if angel.auth_token: ...` with `session = broker_session_manager.any_active_for_broker('angel_one'); if session: quote = angel.get_quote(symbol, exchange)` (the refactored angel_service already pulls the active session internally). Same RCA applies to the startup warning -- the loud WARN should become a no-op or info log since publisher-login means there is no auto-login concept any more.
